headline: Alphabet Stock Joins the Dow but It’s Rising for a Different Reason
summary: The shares were up 1.2% in premarket trading.  The Google-parent is replacing  Verizon Communications  in the 30-stock Dow index from Monday’s session, in a move announced last week.  Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.

content.title: Alphabet Stock Joins the Dow but It’s Rising for a Different Reason

The Google-parent is replacing  Verizon Communications  in the 30-stock Dow index from Monday’s session, in a move announced last week.  Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.

headline: OpenBox AI Partners with CopilotKit to Bring Enterprise-Grade Trust to Agentic AI
summary: OpenBox AI today announced an integration with CopilotKit, the creators of the open AG-UI (Agent-User Interaction) protocol. This partnership solves a critical challenge for enterprises: how to deploy AI agents that are autonomous yet fully governed. By adding OpenBox's runtime governance layer to the AG-UI stack, organizations can now enforce strict compliance policies, mandate human approval for sensitive actions, and generate tamper-evident audit trails — all without slowing down development

content.title: OpenBox AI Partners with CopilotKit to Bring Enterprise-Grade Trust to Agentic AI
content.summary: OpenBox AI today announced an integration with CopilotKit, the creators of the open AG-UI (Agent-User Interaction) protocol. This partnership solves a critical challenge for enterprises: how to deploy AI agents that are autonomous yet fully governed. By adding OpenBox's runtime governance layer to the AG-UI stack, organizations can now enforce strict compliance policies, mandate human approval for sensitive actions, and generate tamper-evident audit trails — all without slowing down development

headline: Comcast, Alphabet, Palantir, SpaceX, Microsoft, and More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market

Semiconductor companies, other artificial-intelligence stocks, and the Magnificent Seven were among those rebounding to start the week.  Microsoft  jumped 2% ahead of the open, while  Amazon Alphabet Nvidia Meta  and  Tesla  were all up around 1%.

headline: Deep Sky Delivers North America's First Certified Direct Air Capture Carbon Removal Credits
summary: Deep Sky and Isometric today announced that carbon captured and permanently removed from the atmosphere by Deep Sky Alpha in Alberta has been independently reviewed and registered as the first certified direct air capture (DAC) carbon removal credits in North America. The credits will be delivered to Microsoft and Royal Bank of Canada (RBC).

content.title: Deep Sky Delivers North America's First Certified Direct Air Capture Carbon Removal Credits
content.summary: Deep Sky and Isometric today announced that carbon captured and permanently removed from the atmosphere by Deep Sky Alpha in Alberta has been independently reviewed and registered as the first certified direct air capture (DAC) carbon removal credits in North America. The credits will be delivered to Microsoft and Royal Bank of Canada (RBC).

thumbnail.caption: Deep Sky Alpha, located in Innisfail, Alberta has now generated North America's first verified DAC carbon removal credits.

headline: Why AI is the core of the 'Back to the Future' trade
summary: US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are showing its first signs of recovery since last week's tech-wide sell-off. Great Hill Capital chairman Thomas Hayes explains to Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi why he is calling AI as being part of the "Back to the Future" trade as investors begin to shrug off more geopolitical headlines centered around oil price and Iran.

content.title: Why AI is the core of the 'Back to the Future' trade

US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are showing its first signs of recovery since last week's tech-wide sell-off.

Great Hill Capital chairman Thomas Hayes explains to Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi why he is calling AI as being part of the "Back to the Future" trade as investors begin to shrug off more geopolitical headlines centered around oil price and Iran.

headline: Alphabet Stock Rallies After Capping Meta’s AI Use, Joining Dow
summary: The Google-parent is replacing  Verizon Communications  in the 30-stock Dow index from Monday’s session, in a move announced last week.  Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.  Alphabet’s move comes amid a broader rebound for the Magnificent Seven group of stocks on Monday.

content.title: Alphabet Stock Rallies After Capping Meta’s AI Use, Joining Dow
content.summary: The Google-parent is replacing  Verizon Communications  in the 30-stock Dow index from Monday’s session, in a move announced last week.  Because the Dow is a price-weighted index—meaning stock price rather than market capitalization determine the weighting of each company—few funds choose to replicate its composition.  Alphabet’s move comes amid a broader rebound for the Magnificent Seven group of stocks on Monday.
